name: L-22
events: 3 Nov 1942 L-22 lays a minefield of the Syltefjord.
7 Nov 1942 While on patrol of the Syltefjord, L-22 fires 4 torpedoes at what is identified as an enemy auxiliary minesweeper. All torpedoes missed their target.

14 Nov 1942 The German auxiliary patrol vessel Schiff 18 / Alteland (419 GRT) hits a mine and sank off the Varangerfjord in position 69°56'N, 30°00'E. The mine was laid by the Soviet submarine L-22 on 3 November 1942.
20 Dec 1942 L-22 lays a minefield off the Bosfjord.
14 Apr 1943 The Norwegian tug Pasvik (238 GRT) hits a mine and sank in position 69°55'N, 30°00'E. The mine was possibly laid on 20 December 1942 by the Soviet submarine L-22.
22 Apr 1943 L-22 lays a minefield in Lopphavet.
24 Apr 1943 While on patrol in Lopphavet, L-22 fires 6 torpedoes at what is identified as a merchant of 8000 GRT. All torpedoes however miss their target.
26 May 1943 L-22 lays a minefield off the Syltefjord.
1 Jun 1943 The German hospital ship Birka (1000 GRT) hits a mine and sank in position 70°20'N, 21°50'E. The mine was laid by the Soviet submarine L-22 on 26 May 1943.
25 Aug 1943 L-22 lays a minefield off Honningsvaag.
1 Sep 1943 L-22 torpedoed and damaged the German merchant R?desheimer (2036 GRT) off the Persfjord.
28 Dec 1943 The German coastal minesweeper R 64 (125 tons) hits a mine and is sunk off Honningsvaag. The mine was laid on 25 August 1943 by the Soviet submarine L-22.
